name: Justice Devendra Kumar Upadhyaya
bio_text: Born on 16th June 1965 at Village Muskarai, District Ambedkar Nagar, Uttar Pradesh. Graduated in laws (LL.B.) in the year 1991 from Lucknow University. Enrolled as an Advocate on 11th May 1991 and joined chambers of his father - Shri R.A. Upadhyaya - practicing at Board of Revenue for last sixty years, Shri Mohd Arif Khan, Senior Advocate and Shri S.K. Kalia, Senior Advocate. Mainly practised in Civil and Constitutional sides at High Court of Judicature at Allahabad, at Lucknow. Was appointed as the Chief Standing Counsel, State of U.P. in May 2007 and continued to hold that office till elevation as Judge of the High Court of Judicature at Allahabad. Appointed as a puisne Judge of the High Court of Judicature at Allahabad on 21st November 2011 and took oath as the permanent Judge of High Court of Judicature at Allahabad on 6th August 2013. Became Senior Judge of the Lucknow Bench on 28th March 2023. Appointed and took oath as the Chief Justice of the High Court of Bombay on 29th July 2023. Transferred as the Chief Justice of the High Court of Delhi and took oath of office on 21 st January, 2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. 'Humour in Court' is an endeavour of the Information Technology & Artificial Intelligence Committee of the High Court of Delhi to capture such moments and preserve them for posterity. name: Justice V. Kameswar Rao
bio_text: Born on 7th August, 1965. Did his BA (Hons.) Geography and LL.B from Delhi University in the years 1987 and 1990 respectively. Awarded Certificate of Merit for standing second in College in the year 1985-86. Enrolled as an Advocate in March, 1991 with Bar Council of Delhi. Practiced in the Supreme Court of India, Delhi High Court, Central Administrative Tribunal (Principal Bench), New Delhi. Also appeared in other High Courts like at Madras High Court, Port Blair (Circuit Bench of Calcutta High Court). Conducted cases relating to Service Law, Labour Law, Arbitration, Constitution and Administrative Law. Represented various Public Sector Undertakings, Banks & Autonomous Bodies. Was Member Executive of the Central Administrative Tribunal Bar Association (Principal Bench), New Delhi. Designated as Senior Advocate by the Delhi High Court in January, 2010. Elevated as Additional Judge of High Court of Delhi on 17th April, 2013 and as Permanent Judge on 18th March, 2015 (F/N) and His Lordship was transferred as a Judge of Karnataka High Court and assumed office on 01.06.2024.  His Lordship assumed charge as the Acting Chief Justice of Karnataka High Court on 30.05.2025. His Lordship repatriated and assumed office as Judge of Delhi High Court on 21.07.2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Nitin Wasudeo Sambre
bio_text: Born on December 19, 1967 in Nagpur, Justice Nitin Wasudeo Sambre did schooling from Somalwar High School, Nagpur, obtained degree in B.Com from Shivaji Science College and earned degree in LL.B. from the University College of Law, Nagpur in 1992.  Enrolled as an Advocate on August 25, 1992 and practiced across civil, writ and criminal matters at all three Benches of the Bombay High Court (Mumbai, Aurangabad, Nagpur). Held key role of Secretary, High Court Bar Association, Nagpur from 2004 till 2007; Government Pleader and Public Prosecutor from 1 st September, 2007 and Standing Counsel for Corporate Houses and Statutory Bodies, such as, Bharat Petroleum, Indian Oil, BHEL and council for bodies like Municipal Councils, Tata Sons and Godrej.  Elevated as an Additional Judge of the Bombay High Court on January 06, 2014 and Permanent Judge on March 02, 2016.   Entrusted with additional responsibility of serving as Chairman of the Bombay High Court Legal Services Sub-Committee, Nagpur Bench;  Member of the Governing Council of the State Judicial Academy, Chairman of the Executive Council of Maharashtra National Law University, Nagpur and active member of the Judicial Officers’ Welfare Committee. Justice Nitin Wasudeo Sambre assumed charge as Judge of Delhi High Court on July 21, 2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Dinesh Mehta
bio_text: Born on 28.01.1968 at Jodhpur, did schooling from Bal Niketan School Jodhpur, obtained degree in B.Com (Hons.) in 1988  and earned degree in Law from Jai Narain Vyas University, Jodhpur in the year 1992.  After getting himself enrolled with Bar Council of Rajasthan in September 1992, joined chamber of his father Sh. B.C. Mehta, Advocate. He advised and appeared for various corporate clients and other litigants in High Court and other Fora in matters relating to Taxation, Arbitration, Banking Laws, Company Laws, Electricity Laws, Environment Laws, Insurance Laws, Agrarian Laws, Municipal Laws.  Handled cases involving Constitutional, Contractual Mining, Election, Civil Service and Industrial disputes.  Was counsel for Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL), Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd. (NPCIL), Kotak Mahindra Bank Ltd. and Panel Lawyer for Oriental Insurance Company Limited, United India Insurance Company Limited and Industries Development Bank of India (IDBI), Indira Gandhi Nahar Pariyojna (IGNP) (1999-2003).  Appeared for National Highways Authority of India (NHAI), Hindustan Zinc Limited, Rajasthan Financial Corporation (RFC), Security and Exchange Board of India (SEBI).  Was elevated as a Judge of Rajasthan High Court on 16.11.2016. Has been the Chairman of Steering Committee (Computer Committee), Committee for publishing Souvenir on completion of 75 years of establishment of Rajasthan High Court, Executive Committee, Rajasthan State Judicial Academy, Member of the General Council, National Law University Jodhpur, was a member of Various Committees of Rajasthan High Court, including Administrative Committee.  Transferred to Hon’ble High Court of Delhi and took oath on 28.10.2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complai

name: Justice Vivek Chaudhary
bio_text: Graduated in Law from Meerut University, Meerut in 1988. Enrolled as an Advocate on Nov 12, 1988. Practised in Civil, Constitutional side at Allahabad High Court and Supreme Court. Elevated as Additional Judge on Feb 20, 2017. Took oath as Permanent Judge on Mar 14, 2018. Transferred as Judge, High Court of Delhi and took Oath on 21.07.2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Prathiba M. Singh
bio_text: Justice Prathiba M. Singh completed her 5-year law course from University Law College, Bangalore. She graduated as the 1st Rank holder from Bangalore University and represented India at the Philip C. Jessup Moot Court competition in Chicago, USA. After her graduation, she was offered the ODASSS scholarship by the Cambridge Commonwealth Trust to study LL.M. at the University of Cambridge (U.K.). She enrolled with the Bar in 1991. Before being elevated to the Bench, she was a leading Intellectual Property lawyer in India. She had the distinction of handling landmark matters in all areas of IPR laws including patents, trademarks, designs,

name: Justice Navin Chawla
bio_text: Born in New Delhi on 07th August 1969. Did his schooling from Delhi Public School, Mathura Road and graduated from Delhi University in B.Com (Hons.) in 1990. Thereafter obtained degree of laws (LL.B) from Campus Law Centre, Delhi University and was enrolled with the Bar Council of Delhi in 1993. Qualified as an Advocate-On-Record of the Supreme Court in 1999. Practiced before the Delhi High Court, Supreme Court, and before various other judicial/quasi-judicial fora in various fields like Constitutional Laws, Civil Suits, Criminal Laws, specializing in Telecom and Broadcasting Laws, Arbitrations etc. Appointed as the Standing Counsel for the Union of India in the Delhi High Court in 2001.  Appointed as Permanent Judge of Delhi High Court on 15th May, 2017.    e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice C. Hari Shankar
bio_text: Born in New Delhi on 4th May 1968, Justice C. Hari Shankar completed his schooling from St. Columba’s School and, thereafter, acquired B. Sc. (Hons) in Chemistry from Kirori Mal College and LL.B. from the Campus Law Centre, Delhi University in 1993. He has appeared, as arguing/senior counsel, before several judicial fora, including, but not limited to, the Supreme Court of India, High Courts of Delhi, Calcutta, Gujarat, Bombay, Allahabad, Punjab & Haryana, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, Uttaranchal, Andhra Pradesh, Madras and Himachal Pradesh, the Central Administrative Tribunal, the Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal, the Appellate Tribunal for Foreign Exchange, the Company Law Board, the AAIFR, the NCDRC, and the National Green Tribunal. His core areas of specialization were indirect taxes, along with allied subjects such as foreign exchange and COFEPOSA, and service law. He was on the Panel of Special Counsel representing the Central Government in the Supreme Court of India, and was also empaneled Counsel for the Directorate General of Anti-Dumping. He was regularly appearing on behalf of the Central Council for Research in Unani Medicine before the CAT and before this Court. On 20th August 2014, he was designated Senior Advocate by the Delhi High Court.  Justice Hari Shankar was appointed permanent Judge of this Court on 15th May 2017.   e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Anil Kshetarpal
bio_text: Hon’ble Mr. Justice Anil Kshetarpal hails from Kurukshetra and was born on 19.11.1964. His father, Sh. S.P. Kshetarpal was a practising Advocate at Kurukshetra. In 1987, after completing LL.B., he enrolled with the Bar Council of Punjab and Haryana. He started practice at Kurukshetra in 1987. Thereafter, in May 1988, he shifted his practice to the Punjab and Haryana High Court and joined the office of Hon’ble Mr. Justice V.K. Bali, Chief Justice (Retd.), who was then a practising Senior Advocate. In 1991, he started his independent practice on the civil and constitutional side. In January 2014, he was designated as a Senior Advocate by the Punjab and Haryana High Court. On 10.07.2017, he was elevated as an Additional Judge of the Punjab and Haryana High Court, Chandigarh, and later appointed as a Permanent Judge on 3.12.2018. He was transferred as Judge, High Court of Delhi and took oath of office on 21.07.2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Avneesh Jhingan
bio_text: Hon’ble Mr. Justice Avneesh Jhingan Date of Birth                                       :                29.01.1969 Educational Qualification                  :                B.Com (Hons.), LL.B. Enrolment as Advocate                     :                1992 Elevation as High Court Judge          :                10.07.2017 Judge, Rajasthan High Court             :                01.11.2023   Hon’ble Mr. Justice Avneesh Jhingan was born on 29.01.1969 in a family of lawyers. Father of His Lordship was a leading lawyer practicing in the Punjab and Haryana High Court.   His Lordship did B.Com (Hons.) from Goswami Ganesh Dutta Sanatan Dharma College, Sector 32-C, Chandigarh, and after completing his graduation in Law from Panjab University, Chandigarh, in the year 1992, was enrolled as an Advocate with the Bar Council for the States of Punjab and Haryana.   His Lordship is a keen Badminton player and participated in various tournaments.  His Lordship as an Advocate conducted cases primarily pertaining to civil and tax matters and was elected as Chief Patron of the Punjab Tax Bar Association.   His Lordship was elevated as an Additional Judge of the Punjab and Haryana High Court on 10.07.2017 and sworn in as Permanent Judge on 03.12.2018.   Transferred to Rajasthan High Court and took oath on 01.11.2023.   His Lordship was transferred as Judge, High Court of Delhi and took oath of office on 28.10.2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Subramonium Prasad
bio_text: Justice Subramonium Prasad graduated in B.Com. (Hons.) from Delhi University. He acquired his LLB degree from Campus Law Centre, Delhi University in the year 1990. Justice Prasad cleared the Advocate-On–Record exam in 1996 and started his independent practice. He became the Standing Counsel for the State of Tamil Nadu in the Supreme Court of India in 2003, a post he held till 2006. He was also the standing counsel for the custodian appointed under the Special Court (Trial of Offences Relating to Transactions in Securities) Act, 1992, dealing with cases pertaining to the 1992 stock exchange scam. In October 2012, Justice Prasad was appointed as the Additional Advocate General for the State of Tamil Nadu in the Supreme Court of India. Justice Prasad was designated as a Senior Advocate by the Supreme Court of India in April 2015. Over his career spanning 28 years, Justice Prasad has worked on several high profile matters, and has been involved in several reported judgements of the Supreme Court on various subjects like Constitutional Law, Tax, Corporate and Commercial Law, Criminal Law, Election Law, Service Law and Inter State River Water Disputes. Justice Prasad was appointed as an Additional Judge of the Madras High Court on 4.06.2018. He was transferred to Delhi High Court as an Additional Judge on 13.03.2020 and confirmed as a Permanent Judge of the Madras High Court on 17.03.2020.   e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Jyoti Singh
bio_text: Born on 1.10.1966, graduated in Botany (Hons.) from Kirori Mal College, Delhi University. Acquired L.L.B. degree from Campus Law Centre, Delhi University.  Enrolled with the Bar Council of Delhi.  Practiced before the Hon’ble Supreme Court, High Court of Delhi and Tribunals. Held the following appointments: • Senior Counsel for the Government of India in the High Court of Delhi • Standing Counsel for UPSC • Standing Counsel for DTC • Standing Counsel for Delhi Government in Central Administrative Tribunal • Additional Standing Counsel for NDMC  • Panel Counsel for Provident Fund, DDA and Canara Bank Was a member of Delhi High Court Legal Services Committee and participated in Lok Adalats and workshops organised by the Committee. Specialised in service matters, particularly Armed Forces and Para-Military Forces. Was appointed as Amicus Curiae in a number of cases by the High Court of Delhi and the Armed Forces Tribunal. Designated as Senior Advocate in 2011 by the High Court of Delhi. Elevated as Judge of High Court of Delhi on 22.10.2018.   e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Tushar Rao Gedela
bio_text: An alumnus of The Army Public School, Dhaula Kuan, New Delhi, Tushar Rao Gedela was born on 18th July 1967. He pursued Political Science (Hons) from Deshbandhu Gupta College, Delhi University followed by BA LLB from Meerut University in 1993. He enrolled with the Delhi Bar Council in the year 1994.  He started his professional journey at the District Courts at Tis Hazari and learnt the nuances of Civil, Criminal and Family side jurisdictions and soon thereafter joined the office of Shri M. Chandrashekharan, former Additional Solicitor General of India in 1997 and handled matters relating to Customs and Excise. Thereafter he was associated with the office of Former Chief Justice of Shimla High Court, Justice M N Rao (Retd.) where he was exposed to Service matters apart from others. He primarily practiced in the Supreme Court of India, Delhi High Court, TDSAT and Armed Forces Tribunal.  He was appointed as Central Government Counsel in Delhi High Court in the year 2000 till 2003 and appointed as Senior Panel Counsel for Central Government at Delhi High Court in 2014, Panel ‘A’ Counsel for the Supreme Court of India in 2015 and retained these positions till his designation as Senior Advocate in the month of March, 2021. Appointed as Permanent Judge of the Delhi High Court on 18/05/2022.    e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Manmeet Pritam Singh Arora
bio_text: Justice Manmeet Pritam Singh Arora was born to a business family in the year 1974. She was raised and educated in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. She attended Mount Carmel High School upto Class Xth and thereafter completed her schooling from St. Xavier’s High School, Loyola Hall in the year 1991.  In the year 1994, she graduated from H.L. College of Commerce, Gujarat University with a B. Com degree. She obtained her LL.B. degree in the year 1997 from Sir L.A. Shah Law College, Gujarat University. After completing her education, she moved to Delhi and enrolled as an Advocate with Bar Council of Delhi in 1997. She practised as an independent litigator and appeared regularly before the High Court of Delhi. She worked extensively in the areas of civil litigation, commercial disputes, company law, arbitrations and thus, practised on the original side of the High Court. She also handled matters relating to administrative law. She appeared before the Supreme Court, different High Courts, National Company Law Tribunal, National Consumer Disputes Redressal Forum, arbitrations and other civil courts as well. During 25 years at the Bar, she has been appointed as Sole Arbitrator, Amicus Curiae and Court Commissioner from time to time by the High Court of Delhi. She has been an external member of the sexual harassment committees of various organisations.  She was appointed as a Permanent Judge of the High Court of Delhi on 18.05.2022. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Sachin Datta
bio_text: Justice Sachin Datta was born on 15 th August, 1973 in Delhi. After completing his schooling from Delhi Public School, RK Puram in 1991, he graduated from St. Stephens College, Delhi University in 1994. He then pursued law and graduated from Campus Law Centre, Delhi University, in 1997 and got enrolled with the Bar Council of Delhi. As a lawyer, he handled a wide variety of cases across practice areas. He represented several prominent public sector undertakings in the Delhi High Court and the Supreme Court of India. He also practised extensively on the original side of the Delhi High Court. He acted as counsel in several landmark litigations in the field of Intellectual Property as also in several prominent International Arbitration matters. Justice Datta was the Central Government Standing Counsel in the Delhi High Court for several years, and as such, represented the Central Government in a wide variety of litigation. He was designated as a Senior Advocate by the High Court of Delhi in August, 2014. He has published various papers and has also been a speaker at various international events. Appointed as a Permanent Judge of the High Court of Delhi on 18 th May, 2022.  e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Saurabh Banerjee
bio_text: Born on 20th January, 1976 in Delhi. He is a second generation lawyer after his father, Mr. Bhawani Shankar Banerjee, Advocate. After completing his schooling from Modern School, Barakhamba Road, New Delhi in 1993 he graduated in B.A. English (Hons.) from the University of Delhi in 1996. He then obtained his LL.B. degree from the Faculty of Law, University of Delhi in 1999 and enrolled as an Advocate with the Bar Council of Delhi in 1999. While practicing, he was dealing in matters relating to intellectual property rights, arbitration, commercial disputes, constitution, property rent, service, family (matrimonial & custody), labour and criminal before the Hon’ble Supreme Court of India, various Hon’ble High Court/s and District Court/s, Forum/s and Tribunal/s across India. He was an Arbitrator, Delhi International Arbitration Centre, Hon’ble High Court of Delhi; a Mediator, Delhi High Court Mediation Centre, Hon’ble High Court of Delhi; Group ‘A’ Panel Advocate, Union of India before the Hon’ble Supreme Court of India; Standing Counsel, National Highways Authority of India before the Hon’ble High Court of Delhi; General Counsel, Hon’ble High Court of Delhi before Hon’ble High Court of Delhi; General Counsel, University of Delhi before Hon’ble High Court of Delhi; General Counsel, Indira Gandhi National Open University before Hon’ble High Court of Delhi; and General Counsel, Delhi High Court Legal Services Committee before Hon’ble High Court of Delhi. He was involved in the landmark judgments relating to Section 364A of Indian Penal Code, 1860 before the Hon’ble High Court of Delhi and Section 52(1) of the

name: Justice Anish Dayal
bio_text: Anish Dayal was born in 1973 at Allahabad (now Prayagraj) and did his initial schooling there at St. Joseph College. He completed schooling from Delhi Public School, Noida, where he was selected as the Head Boy. Thereafter, he graduated in B.Sc.(Hons.) Physics from St. Stephen’s College, Delhi University and then read for his law degree with M.A. (Hons.) at Churchill College, University of Cambridge, U.K., as a commonwealth scholar.  He enrolled as an Advocate with the Delhi Bar Council in September, 1995 and had his independent legal practice primarily before the Supreme Court of India and the High Court of Delhi for over 25 years - predominantly in civil, constitutional and commercial law with special focus in areas of media, entertainment and sports law. He was a visiting faculty resource at many law schools in India and authored the Section on Damages (Vol. 9 Halsbury’s Laws of India) and of other books on sports and media.  He wrote periodically for newspapers and journals and for some time contributed a regular column on law for Live Mint and TheWall Street Journal. He conceived, curated and was part of the Editorial Board of Quarterly Bar Review, a journal of the Delhi High Court Bar Association.He also devoted his time to policy related issues inter aliaon road safety, heritage conservation, environment and sustainability and represented a number of national level athletes on a pro bono basis.  He was designated as a Senior Advocate by the Delhi High Court in March, 2021 and elevated to the Bench on 2nd June, 2022. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Amit Sharma
bio_text: Amit Sharma was born on 7th July, 1972. He completed his Senior Secondary School from Delhi Public School, R.K. Puram, New Delhi in 1989. He completed his Graduation in BA History (H.) from St. Stephen’s College, University of Delhi in 1992. After completing his LL.B from Campus Law Centre, Faculty of Law, University of Delhi in 1995, he got enrolled with the Bar Council of Delhi. He started his profession on the Criminal side appearing before the Hon’ble High Court of Delhi and Trial Courts. In 1999 he was awarded British Chevening Scholarship by Foreign and Commonwealth Office, UK, for Corporate Law at College of Law, York 1999-2000. He was elected as Addl. Secretary, New Delhi Bar Association (2004-2005). He was appointed Additional Public Prosecutor for NCT of Delhi before Hon’ble Delhi High Court (2007-2010). He was appointed as Spl. Public Prosecutor for the National Investigation Agency in 2010. As Spl. PP he handled prosecution relating to national security involving scheduled terrorist organisations. In his capacity as Spl. PP he was also actively involved in extradition proceedings on behalf of NIA. He was elected Member of Bar Council of Delhi in 2009 and held the post of Vice Chairman (2014-2015). As member of Bar Council nominated to the Governing Council and Executive Committee of National Law University, Delhi.  He was elected to the post of Hony. Secretary, Delhi High Court Bar Association (2016-2019).  Nominated as member of Special Committee, Bar Council of Delhi U/S 8A of Advocates Act, 1961 in 2017. He was designated as Senior Advocate by Hon’ble Delhi High Court in March, 2021. Appointed as Additional Judge of High Court of Delhi on 02.06.2022 and became permanent Judge on 06.03.2023. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Com

name: Justice Om Prakash Shukla
bio_text: Born on 20 th April, 1977, Justice Om Prakash Shukla, graduated in Laws (LL.B) in the year 2003 from Lucknow University. Enrolled as an Advocate with U.P. Bar Council in the year 2003. Practiced in Civil, Service, Constitutional, Revenue, Criminal and Miscellaneous matters at the Supreme Court of India, High Court of Judicature at Allahabad, Lucknow, Delhi High Court. Served as Group ‘A’ Counsel for Union of India and Senior Panel Lawyer of State of Uttar Pradesh at Supreme Court of India and also worked as Standing Counsel for ONGC, Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan,  GAIL, NHPC etc.  Appointed as an Additional Judge of the High Court of Judicature at Allahabad, took oath on 3 rd August, 2022 and later took oath as the Permanent Judge on 21 st March, 2024. Transferred as the Judge of the High Court of Delhi and took oath of office on 21 st July, 2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. Amidst the regular humdrum of daily courtroom proceedings, humour props up unexpectedly, sometimes on account of the wit of an Advocate or a Judge and sometimes on account of an innocent remark of a litigant or a witness. 'Humour in Court' is an endeavour of the Information Technology & Artificial Intelligence Committee of the High Court of Delhi to capture such moments and preserve them for posterity. name: Justice Girish Kathpalia
bio_text: After graduating in B.Sc.(Hons.) Zoology from Sri Venkateswara College, Delhi, graduated in LLB from Campus Law Centre, Delhi University in1989. Got enrolled with the Bar Council of Delhi and practiced as Advocate across various jurisdictions in Delhi District Courts, Central Administrative Tribunal, Delhi High Court and Supreme Court. Worked as Govt.Counsel for Govt.of NCT of Delhi as well as for Union of India. Also served as Lecturer(part-time) in Campus Law Centre, Delhi University during 1995-98. Got published more than 200 articles, essays and research papers on various legal, social, scientific and political issues. Compiled and got published booklets on various enactments. Authored book titled: Criminology and Prison Reforms (published by Lexis Nexis). Worked as Chief Editor of “Evidence-1989” (magazine of Campus Law Center, Delhi University) and as Editor of “Nyayapravah” (magazine of AkhilBharatiyaAdhivaktaParishad). Joined as directly recruited Delhi Higher Judicial Service officer in 2002 and presided over different jurisdictions including Additional District Judge, Additional Sessions Judge, Motor Accidents Claims Tribunal, Labour Court and NDPS Court. Also served as Registrar General, Delhi High Court; as Principal Judge, Family Court; as District & Sessions Judge, South-East; and as Principal District & Sessions Judge, Head-Quarter, Delhi. Got elevated as Additional Judge of the Delhi High Court on 1st May, 2023. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Manoj Jain
bio_text: (Date of Birth: 28th December 1964) Graduating at the top from Maharshi Dayanand University in Rohtak, Haryana, earned his LL.B. from Panjab University, Chandigarh, in 1986, once again achieving the highest distinction.  He joined the Delhi Judicial Service in 1992 and was promoted to the Delhi Higher Judicial Service in 2003 and presided over a wide range of jurisdictions, including Civil, Criminal, and Family Law. He is also a Fellow of Commonwealth Judicial Education Institute, Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada. In addition to his judicial roles, he served as the Director (Academics) at the Delhi Judicial Academy, Registrar General of the High Court of Delhi, before his elevation. Sworn in as Additional Judge of Delhi High Court on 1st May, 2023 and as Permanent Judge of Delhi High Court on 22nd July, 2024. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Ravinder Dudeja
bio_text: Justice Ravinder Dudeja was born in Delhi on 17th May, 1966.  He completed his schooling at Kulachi Hansraj Model School in Delhi, and subsequently acquired B.Sc. and an LLB degree from Delhi University. He was enrolled as an Advocate with Bar Council of Delhi in 1989. He began his judicial career in 1992 by joining the Delhi Judicial Services and was promoted to the Delhi Higher Judicial Services in 2003. During his tenure, he served as Metropolitan Magistrate, Civil Judge, Additional District and Sessions Judge, Director/Officiating Chairperson of the Delhi Judicial Academy, and Registrar General of the Delhi High Court. He also presided over the first paperless e-court of the country at the District level. Elevated as an Additional Judge, High Court of Delhi on 20th October, 2023. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Ajay Digpaul
bio_text: Justice Ajay Digpaul was born into a family of lawyers. His father, Shri Jagdish Chandra Digpaul, was a well-known criminal lawyer. He completed his schooling from Hansraj Model School, Punjabi Bagh, New Delhi. He then graduated from Hindu College and earned his LLB degree from Law Centre, both affiliated with Delhi University. Ajay Digpaul's professional career began in 1993 when he enrolled with the Bar Council of Delhi. He quickly gained recognition, becoming a Standing Counsel for the Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) and the Union of India. In recognition of his exceptional skills and dedication in the field of law, Justice Ajay Digpaul was elevated as a Judge of the High Court of Delhi on January 8, 2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Harish Vaidyanathan Shankar
bio_text: Born on February 18, 1974, he did his schooling from Sardar Patel Vidyalaya, Delhi and Bharatiya Vidya Bhawan, Kodaikanal. He is an alumnus of St. Joseph’s College of Arts & Science, Bangalore [B.A. (Eco, Pol Sc., Socio)]. He completed his LL.B from Campus Law Centre, University of Delhi in 1998.  He has been associated with the chambers of Mr. K. K. Venugopal, Mr. P.H. Parekh and Mr. Soli J. Sorabjee, the then Attorney General for India. He worked briefly in the (then) law firm of Udwadia, Udeshi & Berjis at Bombay before moving to England to earn his LL.M. in International Economic Law from the University of Warwick. He was also associated briefly with the Delhi office of the law firm, Mulla & Mulla, Craigie, Blunt & Caroe.  Before being elevated to the Bench, he worked extensively in matters relating to varied and diversified fields of law, inter alia, IPR, Service, Arbitration, Company and Criminal Laws. He has also appeared in various High Courts and Tribunals across India. He has served as a Panel A counsel in the Supreme Court of India, a Central Government Standing Counsel in the Delhi High Court, a Senior Panel Counsel in the Armed Forces Tribunal (PB), Special Counsel for Telangana in Krishna Water Dispute and Special Public Prosecutor (For SIT) in the Godhra Riots Matter. He has been part of seminal matters such as the Ram Janmabhoomi matter, Challenge to the Finance Act, 2017 (appointments and functioning of the Tribunals), Karnataka Mining ban cases, disqualification of MLAs cases in Madras High Court etc.     He is certified as a mediator by the IICA & the BIMACC and was a member of the DHCLSC Mediation Panel. He was awarded the silk by the Hon’ble Delhi High Court on 29.11.2024 and appointed as Judge of the High Court of Delhi from 08.01.2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Te

name: Justice Tejas Karia
bio_text: Born on 1 February 1978 in Ahmedabad, Gujarat and belongs to a family of Judges and Lawyers. Holds LLB from ILS Law College, University of Pune (2000), LLM from University of Gujarat in Corporate Laws (2002) and LLM from London School of Economics in International Arbitration and Information Technology Laws (2003).  Started practicing at Ahmedabad City Civil Court in 2000 and gained first-hand experience in trial court proceedings. Practiced in High Court of Gujarat from 2001 and handled Writ Petitions, Letters Patent Appeals, Civil and Criminal Appeals. Conducted a Court Martial as special defense counsel appointed by Indian Army. Appeared before Revenue Tribunal, Central Administrative Tribunal, Debt Recovery Tribunal, State Consumer Commission and Family Court.  Shifted practice to High Court of Delhi in 2004 and joined Amarchand Mangaldas & Co. Appeared before High Courts across India, erstwhile Company Law Board, Securities Appellate Tribunal, National Company Law Tribunals, National Company Law Appellate Tribunal, National Green Tribunal, National Consumer Redressal Commission, Appellate Tribunal for Electricity and Supreme Court of India in landmark cases. Specialized in field of International and Domestic Commercial  Arbitrations and handled complex, high-value ad-hoc and institutional arbitrations across diverse industry sectors having seats in India, London and Singapore as a Counsel before Arbitration Tribunals and the Courts in the matters involving appointment of arbitrators, interim measures, challenge and enforcement of Arbitration Awards. Headed Arbitration practice as Partner at Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas & Co and was appointed as an Arbitrator in Institutional Arbitrations.  Handled matters relating to Information Technology Law,  Privacy and Data Protection, Intermediary Liability, E-Commerce, Cybersecurity, E-Payments, Online safety and content regulations. Held honorary positions as Member, Court of Arbitration of SIAC, ICC Commission on Arbitr

name: Justice Renu Bhatnagar
bio_text: Born in Gurugram, Haryana on 27 th August, 1967, Justice Renu Bhatnagar graduated from M.D.U. Rohtak, Haryana in 1986 and chose to pursue Law as a profession. She completed her L.L.B. from Campus Law Centre, University of Delhi and L.L.M. from Kurukshetra University.  Justice Bhatnagar joined the Delhi Judicial Service in 1992 and was promoted to the Delhi Higher Judicial Service in August, 2003. She was promoted as Principal District Judge and posted as such at South-East District, Saket Courts, Delhi from May, 2023 to June, 2023 and thereafter at Shahdara District, Karkardooma Courts, Delhi from 8 th June, 2023 to 20 th February, 2025. During her long service tenure, she presided over courts dealing with Civil and Criminal Jurisdictions. She held designated courts dealing in Labour and Industrial Laws & Matrimonial disputes, Protection of Children against Sexual Offences Act (POCSO), TADA, POTA and MCOCA, apart from regular Sessions Court and District Court.  Justice Bhatnagar is a trained judicial mediator.  Justice Bhatnagar was elevated as permanent Judge of the High Court of Delhi on 21 st February, 2025 marking a significant milestone in her distinguished career in the judiciary. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Rajneesh Kumar Gupta
bio_text: Born on 21st May, 1966, Justice Rajneesh Kumar Gupta pursued his legal education with distinction, completing his LL.B from Panjab University, Chandigarh, and his LL.M from Kurukshetra University. Justice Rajneesh Kumar Gupta began his professional journey as an Advocate, practicing for three years at the District Courts in Sangrur, Punjab. In 1992, he joined the Delhi Judicial Services, where he demonstrated remarkable legal acumen. His dedication to justice and exemplary service led to his promotion to the Delhi Higher Judicial Service in 2006. Throughout his judicial career, Justice Rajneesh Kumar Gupta has served in a variety of jurisdictions, including Civil, Criminal, Rent, and CBI matters, where he gained extensive experience and expertise. He also served as Principal Judge, Family Court (HQ) and the Principal District & Sessions Judge, (South-East), Saket Courts Complex. Additionally, Justice Rajneesh Kumar Gupta served as the Judge-in-Charge of the Delhi Mediation Centre at Rohini Court, contributing significantly to the promotion of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms. As a trained mediator, he has successfully mediated and settled numerous cases, helping parties achieve amicable resolutions. On 21st February 2025, Justice Rajneesh Kumar Gupta was elevated to the position of Permanent Judge of the High Court of Delhi, marking a significant milestone in his distinguished career in the judiciary.  e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Vinod Kumar
bio_text: Born on 30 th December 1965, Justice Vinod Kumar obtained his degree of Bachelor in Arts from M.L.N. College Yamuna Nagar (Haryana). He completed his LL.B. from Kurukshetra University in 1987 and started his practice at District Courts, Faridabad.  Justice Vinod Kumar was selected for the Delhi Judicial Service in 1992 and was promoted to the Delhi Higher Judicial Service in 2003.  During his service tenure spanning over a period of 32 years, Justice Vinod Kumar has served in various capacities viz. Metropolitan Magistrate at Tis Hazari and Patiala House; Civil Judge at Tis Hazari; Additional Chief Metropolitan Magistrate at Karkardooma; Additional Sessions Judge at Patiala House and Tis Hazari; Spl. Judge, PC Act, CBI at Rohini and Patiala House; District Judge (Commercial Courts) at North-West District, Rohini; Principal Judge, Family Court at South District, Saket; Principal Judge Family Court (HQ) at Dwarka; Principal District & Sessions Judge at West District, Tis Hazari and Principal District & Sessions Judge at East District, Karkardooma. He is a trained mediator and has interest in literature.  Justice Vinod Kumar was elevated as permanent judge of the High Court of Delhi on 24 th July 2025 marking a new chapter in his extensive service in the judiciary.  e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Shail Jain
bio_text: Born on October 7, 1966, Justice Shail Jain began her academic journey with a B.Sc. (Hons.) in Zoology from Delhi University, graduating in 1986. Drawn to the study of law and public service, she pursued her LL.B. at the Campus Law Centre, Delhi University, completing it in 1990. In 1992, Justice Shail Jain cleared the Delhi Judicial Services Examination in her first attempt, marking the start of a distinguished judicial career. While serving as an Additional District Judge in the Delhi Judiciary, she went on to earn her LL.M. from Kurukshetra University, reflecting her commitment to continuous learning. She served with distinction as the Principal District & Sessions Judge for three years before being elevated to the High Court of Delhi on July 24, 2025. Taking oath as a Judge was a defining moment in a career built on integrity, fairness, and dedication to the law. Beyond her judicial work, Justice Shail Jain has contributed to legal scholarship and literature. She is the co-author of the legal guide Patent Agent Examination, published by Universal Books, and the author of ‘ तमन्नाये ,’ a collection of Hindi poetry. As Chairperson of the Delhi District Court’s Hindi Samiti, she has led initiatives to promote Indian languages in legal and cultural spheres. Her poetry has been featured in several Kavi Sammelans and Kavya Goshtis, where it is appreciated for its sincerity and lyrical quality. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Madhu Jain
bio_text: Justice Madhu Jain was born on 18 th November 1966. She completed B.Sc. (Hons.) in Zoology from Daulat Ram College, University of Delhi and thereafter she enrolled at the Campus Law Centre to pursue a degree in Law. She graduated in 1991 and in 1992 she got selected in the Delhi Judicial Services and started serving on the Bench side of the courtroom. She also completed her LL.M. in the year 2000. Owing to her remarkable legal acumen and dedication to serving justice, she got promoted to the Delhi Higher Judicial Services in the year 2003. Across the span of more than three decades at the District Courts, she presided over various Courts catering to a wide spectrum of jurisdictions including but not limited to Civil, Criminal, NDPS, CBI, MACT and Family Courts where her exemplary knowledge and devotion to justice led to speedy disposal of matters and benefit to the society at large. She also served as Principal District and Sessions Judge in different districts of Delhi, i.e. at North, South-East, South and West Districts. She got elevated as a Judge of the High Court of Delhi on 24.07.2025 marking a significant milestone in her distinguished career in the Judiciary. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour. name: Justice Vimal Kumar Yadav
bio_text: Born on 09 th January, and after completing his schooling from Ludlow Castle in 1982 earned his B.COM (HONS) in 1985, L.L.B in 1988 and L.L.M in 2006 apart from having a PG Diploma in Cyber Laws and Internet. He joined Delhi Judicial Service in 1992 and promoted to Delhi Higher Judicial Service in 2003. Apart from presiding over various jurisdictions as Civil Judge, Metropolitan Magistrate, Addl. District & Sessions Judge, Special Judge (PC Act), Principal Judge, Family Court, shouldered the responsibilities as Principal District and Sessions Judge in North District, North-West District at Rohini Court Complex and in New Delhi District at Patiala House Court Complex, New Delhi. As Additional Registrar worked in Hon’ble the Supreme Court of India on deputation between 2008 to 2010. As a trained Mediator, contributed to the settlement of even some complex cases pending for decades. Apart from contributing in some journals, two books have been published i.e poetry compilations.   Justice Vimal Kumar Yadav was elevated as permanent Judge of Delhi High Court on 12 th August, 2025. e-Visitor Pass e-Library Accessibility Services e-Sewa Kendra & Accessibility Helpline निर्णय हिंदी Forms & Proformas Monthly Report on MPs/MLAs Cases in District Court Humour in Court WhatsApp Services FAQ's Telephone Directory Live Streaming Sexual Harassment Complaints High Court of Delhi Download App From Humour in Court Courtrooms are a fertile source of humour.
